sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: ἥκω Διὸς παῖς τήνδε Θηβαίων χθόνα
sections.translation: I , the son of Zeus , have come to this land of the Thebans — Dionysus , whom once Semele , Kadmos ’ daughter , bore , delivered by a lightning-bearing flame . And having taken a mortal form instead of a god ’ s ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: πάρειμι Δίρκης νάματʼ Ἰσμηνοῦ θʼ ὕδωρ .
sections.translation: I am here at the fountains of Dirke and the water of Ismenus . And I see the tomb of my thunder-stricken mother here near the palace , and the remnants of her house , smouldering with the still living flame of Zeus ’ fire , the everlasting insult of Hera against my mother .

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: αἰνῶ δὲ Κάδμον , ἄβατον ὃς πέδον τόδε
sections.translation: I praise Kadmos , who has made this place hallowed , the shrine of his daughter ; and I have covered it all around with the cluster-bearing leaf of the vine . I have left the wealthy lands of the Lydians and Phrygians , the sun-parched plains of the Persians ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: Βάκτριά τε τείχη τήν τε δύσχιμον χθόνα
sections.translation: and the Bactrian walls , and have passed over the wintry land of the Medes , and blessed Arabia , and all of Asia which lies along the coast of the salt sea with its beautifully-towered cities full of Hellenes and barbarians mingled together ;

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: ἐς τήνδε πρῶτον ἦλθον Ἑλλήνων πόλιν ,
sections.translation: and I have come to this Hellene city first , having already set those other lands to dance and established my mysteries there , so that I might be a deity manifest among men . In this land of Hellas , I have first excited Thebes to my cry , fitting a fawn-skin to my body and

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: θύρσον τε δοὺς ἐς χεῖρα , κίσσινον βέλος ·
sections.translation: taking a thyrsos in my hand , a weapon of ivy . For my mother ’ s sisters , the ones who least should , claimed that I , Dionysus , was not the child of Zeus , but that Semele had conceived a child from a mortal father and then ascribed the sin of her bed to Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: Κάδμου σοφίσμαθʼ , ὧν νιν οὕνεκα κτανεῖν
sections.translation: a trick of Kadmos ’, for which they boasted that Zeus killed her , because she had told a false tale about her marriage . Therefore I have goaded them from the house in frenzy , and they dwell in the mountains , out of their wits ; and I have compelled them to wear the outfit of my mysteries .

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: καὶ πᾶν τὸ θῆλυ σπέρμα Καδμείων , ὅσαι
sections.translation: And all the female offspring of Thebes , as many as are women , I have driven maddened from the house , and they , mingled with the daughters of Kadmos , sit on roofless rocks beneath green pines . For this city must learn , even if it is unwilling ,

sections.label: Section 40
sections.translation: that it is not initiated into my Bacchic rites , and that I plead the case of my mother , Semele , in appearing manifest to mortals as a divinity whom she bore to Zeus . 43 Now Kadmos has given his honor and power to Pentheus , his daughter ’ s son ,

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ὃς θεομαχεῖ τὰ κατʼ ἐμὲ καὶ σπονδῶν ἄπο
sections.translation: who fights against the gods as far as I am concerned and drives me away from sacrifices , and in his prayers makes no mention of me , for which I will show him and all the Thebans that I was born a god . And when I have set matters here right , I will move on to another land ,

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: δεικνὺς ἐμαυτόν · ἢν δὲ Θηβαίων πόλις
sections.translation: revealing myself . But if ever the city of Thebes should in anger seek to drive the the Bacchae down from the mountains with arms , I , the general of the Maenads , will join battle with them . On which account I have changed my form to a mortal one and altered my shape into the nature of a man .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ λιποῦσαι Τμῶλον ἔρυμα Λυδίας ,
sections.translation: But , you women who have left Tmolus , the bulwark of Lydia , my sacred band , whom I have brought from among the barbarians as assistants and companions to me , take your drums , native instruments of the city of the Phrygians , the invention of mother Rhea and myself ,

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: βασίλειά τʼ ἀμφὶ δώματʼ ἐλθοῦσαι τάδε
sections.translation: and going about this palace of Pentheus beat them , so that Kadmos ’ city may see . I myself will go to the folds of Kithairon , where the Bacchae are , to share in their dances .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.translation: From the land of Asia ,

sections.label: Section 65
sections.translation: having left sacred Tmolus , I am swift to perform for Bromius my sweet labor and toil easily borne , celebrating the god Bacchus [*] . Who is in the way ? Who is in the way ? Who ? Let him get out of the way indoors , and let everyone keep his mouth pure [*] ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.translation: speaking propitious things . For I will celebrate Dionysus with hymns according to eternal custom .

sections.label: Section 1.73
sections.translation: Blessed is he who , being fortunate and knowing the rites of the gods , keeps his life pure and

sections.label: Section 1.75
sections.translation: has his soul initiated into the Bacchic revels , dancing in inspired frenzy over the mountains with holy purifications , and who , revering the mysteries of great mother Kybele ,

sections.label: Section 1.80
sections.translation: brandishing the thyrsos , garlanded with ivy , serves Dionysus . Go , Bacchae , go , Bacchae , escorting the god Bromius , child of a god ,

sections.label: Section 1.85
sections.translation: from the Phrygian mountains to the broad streets of Hellas — Bromius ,

sections.label: Section 1.88
sections.translation: Whom once , in the compulsion of birth pains ,

sections.label: Section 1.90
sections.translation: the thunder of Zeus flying upon her , his mother cast from her womb , leaving life by the stroke of a thunderbolt . Immediately Zeus , Kronos ’ son ,

sections.label: Section 1.95
sections.translation: received him in a chamber fit for birth , and having covered him in his thigh shut him up with golden clasps , hidden from Hera . And he brought forth , when the Fates

sections.label: Section 1.100
sections.translation: had perfected him , the bull-horned god , and he crowned him with crowns of snakes , for which reason Maenads cloak their wild prey over their locks .

sections.label: Section 2.105
sections.translation: O Thebes , nurse of Semele , crown yourself with ivy , flourish , flourish with the verdant yew bearing sweet fruit , and crown yourself in honor of Bacchus with branches of oak

sections.label: Section 2.110
sections.translation: or pine . Adorn your garments of spotted fawn-skin with fleeces of white sheep , and sport in holy games with insolent thyrsoi [*] . At once all the earth will dance —

sections.label: Section 2.115
sections.translation: whoever leads the sacred band is Bromius — to the mountain , to the mountain , where the crowd of women waits , goaded away from their weaving by Dionysus .

sections.label: Section 2.120
sections.translation: O secret chamber of the Kouretes and you holy Cretan caves , parents to Zeus , where the Korybantes with triple helmet invented for me in their caves this circle ,

sections.label: Section 2.125
sections.translation: covered with stretched hide ; and in their excited revelry they mingled it with the sweet-voiced breath of Phrygian pipes and handed it over to mother Rhea , resounding with the sweet songs of the Bacchae ;

sections.label: Section 2.130
sections.translation: nearby , raving Satyrs were fulfilling the rites of the mother goddess , and they joined it to the dances of the biennial festivals , in which Dionysus rejoices .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ἡδὺς ἐν ὄρεσιν , ὅταν ἐκ θιάσων δρομαί-
sections.translation: He is sweet in the mountains [*] , whenever after the running dance he falls on the ground , wearing the sacred garment of fawn skin , hunting the blood of the slain goat , a raw-eaten delight , rushing to the

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: νος ἐς ὄρεα Φρύγια , Λύδιʼ , ὁ δʼ ἔξαρχος Βρόμιος ,
sections.translation: Phrygian , the Lydian mountains , and the leader of the dance is Bromius , evoe ! [*] The plain flows with milk , it flows with wine , it flows with the nectar of bees .

sections.label: Section 145
sections.translation: The Bacchic one , raising the flaming torch of pine on his thyrsos , like the smoke of Syrian incense , darts about , arousing the wanderers with his racing and dancing , agitating them with his shouts ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: τρυφερόν τε πλόκαμον εἰς αἰθέρα ῥίπτων .
sections.translation: casting his rich locks into the air . And among the Maenad cries his voice rings deep : [*] Go , Bacchae , go , Bacchae , with the luxury of Tmolus that flows with gold ,

sections.label: Section 155
sections.translation: sing of Dionysus , beneath the heavy beat of drums , celebrating in delight the god of delight with Phrygian shouts and cries ,

sections.label: Section 160
sections.translation: when the sweet-sounding sacred pipe sounds a sacred playful tune suited

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: φοιτάσιν εἰς ὄρος εἰς ὄρος · ἡδομέ-
sections.translation: to the wanderers , to the mountain , to the mountain ! And the Bacchante , rejoicing like a foal with its grazing mother , rouses her swift foot in a gamboling dance .

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: τίς ἐν πύλαισι ; Κάδμον ἐκκάλει δόμων ,
sections.translation: Who is at the gates ? Call from the house Kadmos , son of Agenor , who leaving the city of Sidon built this towering city of the Thebans . Let someone go and announce that Teiresias is looking for him . He knows why I have come and

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ἅ τε ξυνεθέμην πρέσβυς ὢν γεραιτέρῳ ,
sections.translation: what agreement I , an old man , have made with him , older still : to twine the thyrsoi , to wear fawn-skins , and to crown our heads with ivy branches .

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ , ὡς σὴν γῆρυν ᾐσθόμην κλύων
sections.translation: Dearest friend , for inside the house I heard and recognized your wise voice , the voice of a wise man ;

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: ἥκω δʼ ἕτοιμος τήνδʼ ἔχων σκευὴν θεοῦ ·
sections.translation: I have come prepared with this equipment of the god . For we must extol him , the child of my daughter , Dionysus , who has appeared as a god to men as much as is in our power . Where must I dance , where set my feet

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: καὶ κρᾶτα σεῖσαι πολιόν ; ἐξηγοῦ σύ μοι
sections.translation: and shake my grey head ? Show me the way , Teiresias , one old man leading another ; for you are wise . And so I shall never tire night or day striking the ground with the thyrsos . Gladly I have forgotten that I am old .

sections.label: Section 189b
sections.translation: Then you and I have the same feelings ,

sections.label: Section 190
sections.translation: for I too feel young and will try to dance .

sections.label: Section 191
sections.translation: Then will we go to the mountain in a chariot ?

sections.label: Section 192
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐχ ὁμοίως ἂν ὁ θεὸς τιμὴν ἔχοι .
sections.translation: But then the god would not have equal honor .

sections.label: Section 193
sections.translation: I , an old man , will lead you , an old man , like a pupil .

sections.label: Section 194
sections.original: ὁ θεὸς ἀμοχθὶ κεῖσε νῷν ἡγήσεται .
sections.translation: The god will lead us there without trouble .

sections.label: Section 195
sections.translation: Are we the only ones in the city who will dance in Bacchus ’ honor ?

sections.label: Section 196
sections.original: μόνοι γὰρ εὖ φρονοῦμεν , οἱ δʼ ἄλλοι κακῶς .
sections.translation: Yes , for we alone think rightly , the rest wrongly .

sections.label: Section 197
sections.original: μακρὸν τὸ μέλλειν · ἀλλʼ ἐμῆς ἔχου χερός .
sections.translation: The delay is long ; come , take hold of my hand .

sections.label: Section 198
sections.translation: Here , take hold , and join your hand with mine .

sections.label: Section 199
sections.original: οὐ καταφρονῶ ʼγὼ τῶν θεῶν θνητὸς γεγώς .
sections.translation: Having been born mortal I do not scorn the gods .

sections.label: Section 200
sections.translation: We mortals have no cleverness in the eyes of the the gods . [*] Our ancestral traditions , and those which we have held throughout our lives , no argument will overturn , not even if some craftiness should be discovered by the depths of our wits . [*] Will anyone say that I do not respect old age ,

sections.label: Section 205
sections.translation: being about to dance with my head covered in ivy ? No , for the god has made no distinction as to whether it is right for men young or old to dance , but wishes to have common honors from all and to be extolled , setting no one apart .

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: ἐπεὶ σὺ φέγγος , Τειρεσία , τόδʼ οὐχ ὁρᾷς ,
sections.translation: Since you do not see this light , Teiresias , I will be your interpreter . Pentheus , child of Echion , to whom I gave control of this land , is coming here to the house now in haste . How fluttered he is ! What new matter will he tell us ?

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: ἔκδημος ὢν μὲν τῆσδʼ ἐτύγχανον χθονός ,
sections.translation: I happened to be at a distance from this land , when I heard of strange evils throughout this city , that the women have left our homes in contrived Bacchic rites , and rush about in the shadowy mountains , honoring with dances

sections.label: Section 220
sections.translation: this new deity Dionysus , whoever he is . I hear that mixing-bowls stand full in the midst of their assemblies , and that they each creep off different ways into secrecy to serve the beds of men , on the pretext that they are Maenads worshipping ;

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: τὴν δʼ Ἀφροδίτην πρόσθʼ ἄγειν τοῦ Βακχίου .
sections.translation: but they consider Aphrodite before Bacchus . As many of them as I have caught , servants keep in the public strongholds with their hands bound , and as many as are absent I will hunt from the mountains , I mean Ino and Agave , who bore me to Echion , and

sections.label: Section 230
sections.translation: Autonoe , the mother of Actaeon . And having bound them in iron fetters , I will soon stop them from this ill-working revelry . And they say that some stranger has come , a sorcerer , a conjuror from the Lydian land ,
